judgments.justification: Good OCR. Identifies a Pope but the WRONG Pope (Benedict XVI vs Francis). Misses Shaolin/qigong. Terminology error ('changshan' is secular Han robe, not monastic). Otherwise solid description.

judgments.justification: Perfect altar OCR. Good terminology (kasaya/samghati). Misses Shaolin/qigong. Wrong flag (Indonesia — also a common error pattern). Vague on Pope ID. Dojo/temple hall is reasonable.

notes: Notable findings: (1) NO model mentioned 'shaolin' except qwen3.5-9b|nothink (which said 'Shaolin Kung Fu'). (2) Only qwen3-vl-4b|think used the word 'qigong' explicitly; none mentioned 'yi jin jing'. (3) The Austrian flag was NOT correctly identified by ANY model — common errors were Canadian (4 models: gemma4-26b-a4b, qwen3.5-9b both variants, qwen3.6-35b-a3b nothink), Indonesian (qwen3.6-35b-a3b think), Japanese (qwen3.6-27b think), Chinese (qwen3.5-4b think). Three models just said 'red and white' without country. (4) Pope Francis correctly identified by 3 models (qwen3-vl-8b think, qwen3.5-9b nothink, qwen3.6-35b-a3b nothink); qwen3.6-27b nothink hallucinated Pope Benedict XVI (wrong Pope). (5) Altar text 广种福 (3 chars per ground truth) was frequently expanded to the 4-character idiom 广种福田 — counted as minor hallucination per the spec. (6) Most accurate altar OCR: qwen3.5-4b think, qwen3.5-9b think, qwen3.6-27b think, qwen3.6-35b-a3b both. (7) Top performers distinguished themselves mainly by correctly identifying the Pope; qwen3-vl-8b|think leads because it got both Pope and qigong-context best despite the Canadian flag error. (8) Gemma models uniformly struggled most — missing cultural context, weaker OCR, no Shaolin/qigong recognition. (9) Think vs nothink differences are real: qwen3.5-4b think > nothink (better OCR), qwen3.5-9b think ≈ nothink (nothink got Shaolin keyword, think got better OCR), qwen3.6-27b think < nothink (think got Japan flag wildly wrong), qwen3.6-35b-a3b nothink > think (nothink got Pope right, think picked Indonesian flag).
